Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The user wants me to analyze a temporal profile model for an insurance claim handling process and 1 Identify anomalies 2 Generate hypotheses on why they exist 3 Propose SQL verification approaches

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For a process that goes R A E P this skips intermediate steps

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: 25 hours is suspiciously round uniform STDEV of 1 hour on a 25 hour mean is very tight coefficient of variation 4 percent The low variance suggests a rigid schedule possibly an automated batch job

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Very long delay between approval and notification High variance suggests inconsistency

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This is suspicious because A should be followed by E and P before C 2 hours is too short for full evaluation and approval Suggests premature closure

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Very fast transition May indicate skipping of approval step or automated system Also skips the P Approve step which should come between E and N

Name: Pattern Recognition - IND
Text: R to E Average 1 day STDEV 8 hours This is reasonable E to C Average 1 hour STDEV 50 minutes High variance relative to mean N to C Average 30 minutes STDEV 5 minutes Seems reasonable

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: R to P low STDEV Suggests an automated or batch process that approves claims at a fixed interval perhaps a daily batch job Could also be artificially inflated or rounded timestamps

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: P to N long delay Manual notification process perhaps customer communication is sent in batches or depends on adjuster workload

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: A to C short Some claims may be denied rejected quickly without full evaluation or there may be data quality issues where C events are logged prematurely

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: E to N very short with low STDEV Could indicate an automated notification system that triggers immediately after evaluation possibly bypassing the approval step

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: I need to write PostgreSQL queries that Calculate actual time differences between events Filter for anomalies Correlate with adjusters claim types regions Identify specific claims violating expected patterns

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Query to find claims with RP time outside expected range around 25 hours STDEV 1 hour means the range is roughly 22 to 28 hours using ZETA 3 Query to find claims where PN exceeds 7 days plus 2 times STDEV Query to find claims where AC is suspiciously short less than say 1 hour without seeing E and P in between Query to find claims where EN is suspiciously short Correlation queries with adjusters claim types resources

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: For the SQL queries Ill use PostgreSQL features like LAG window functions EXTRACT EPOCH FROM for time differences JOINs with claims and adjusters tables Subqueries and CTEs

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Let me structure my response with 1 Identified Anomalies 2 Hypotheses 3 Verification SQL Queries I should present these independently as requested
